description: PHIL-21 S430 challenge: re-classify 20 randomly sampled L3-tagged lessons
challenge_criterion: If >40% of L3-tagged lessons misclassified, level tag is Goodharted
method: Random sample of 20 L3-tagged lessons, each independently classified using L-895 original criteria (L2=measurement/audit/counting primary, L3=strategy/architecture/paradigm primary). Strict threshold: finding 'architectural implications' in a measurement lesson does not make it L3.
expect: 30-50% misclassification based on S430 prediction and agent-inflation observed during classification

actual.common_misclassification_pattern: Measurement/audit lessons tagged L3 because they contain prescriptive rules or identify named patterns, but primary content is quantitative finding
actual.agent_vs_human_disagreement: Automated classifier (explore agent) rated 20/20 as L3 (100%); manual reclassification found 9/20 are L2 (45%). Agent inflates by finding 'architectural implications' in any lesson.

diff: Expected 30-50%: GOT 45%. Threshold 40% EXCEEDED. Agent classification inflates L3 tags — confirms Goodhart mechanism: any lesson can be rationalized as L3 post-hoc.
verdict: PHIL-21 S430 challenge CONFIRMED: level tags are Goodharted at 45% misclassification rate. Automated classification produces 100% L3 (agent inflation). Manual strict classification: 55% genuinely L3. True L3+ rate is ~55% of tagged, not 100%.

implications.true_L3_rate: 55% of tagged = ~12% of all lessons (vs tagged 21.8%)
implications.goodhart_mechanism: Level tag applied by lesson author who wants L3 credit; no adversarial review
implications.fix: Require independent second-classifier or structural L3 criterion (e.g., must produce tool/architectural change, not just identify pattern)
